## Highlights

`feishu-md-sync@0.6.2` pairs with `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1` to harden eventually consistent Code and Whiteboard writes without weakening scoped verification, partial-write evidence, or protected-resource safety.

- Retry only the readback phase after a successful Code create; never replay the provider mutation.
- Keep exact created-block and partial-write evidence when Code readback does not converge.
- Treat image-to-Whiteboard replacement as a compound provider mutation whose verified final revision may advance beyond the intermediate revision returned by Feishu.
- Continue to reject lagging Whiteboard readback, unrelated document drift, changed sibling identity, or mismatched raw Whiteboard content.
- Accept schema-v1 prepared batches and recovery checkpoints produced by engine 0.1.0 under engine 0.1.1.
- Preserve tracked Supademos only when `isShowcase`, baseline placement, semantic locators, block identity, token, and remote shape remain exact.
- Allow npm attestations to propagate before failing provenance verification, while retaining exact package integrity, tag, commit, workflow, and Sigstore checks.

## Installation

Install the CLI and matching Agent Skill from the same release:

```bash
npm install --global feishu-md-sync@0.6.2
npx skills add 'liyun95/feishu-md-sync#v0.6.2' --skill feishu-md-sync --global --yes
```

Library consumers can install the engine directly:

```bash
npm install --save-exact feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1
```

The v0.6 Skill requires `feishu-md-sync >=0.6.0 <0.7.0`.

## Upgrade compatibility

- `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1` prepares new batches with engine version 0.1.1.
- It also accepts untampered schema-v1 prepared batches and recovery checkpoints produced by 0.1.0.
- Future engine versions, unsupported schema versions, fingerprint mismatches, stale revisions, stale snapshots, and unrelated remote drift remain hard stops before mutation or recovery continuation.
- `feishu-md-sync@0.6.2` pins engine 0.1.1 exactly; applications that persist prepared engine batches may upgrade without discarding valid 0.1.0 state.

## Safety model

- The engine does not own Markdown planning, confirmation flags, receipt policy, or automatic retry decisions.
- Code retry is limited to verified post-create readback and never repeats the successful create call.
- Whiteboard revision tolerance applies only to image replacement after exact structural and raw-resource verification; native table revision barriers remain exact.
- No engine or CLI path automatically falls back to document replacement.
- Partial-write evidence remains a stop state requiring fresh status, diff, dry-run, and ordinary confirmation gates.

## Release ordering

The protected workflow publishes `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1` first from `v0.6.2`, verifies byte identity and provenance, installs the CLI candidate in an isolated consumer using the registry-resolved engine, and only then publishes `feishu-md-sync@0.6.2`.

## Known boundaries

- Live write verification uses the dedicated controlled Feishu document and runs serially.
- Automatic merge remains available only for `gfm`; authoring dialects require independent pull and manual reconciliation.
- Code blocks that depend on Feishu synced-block behavior remain manually provisioned and protected; the shared engine does not synthesize that upstream feature.
- Project B should update its exact engine pin only after both 0.1.1 and 0.6.2 registry artifacts and provenance have been verified.
